programmes.show_description: Hello Sun: Fran tries to discover her inner karma at the advice of her disapproving friend Eva. Meanwhile, Bernard finds positive proof that Manny is mad.
A Nice Change: Fran has suggested that she, Bernard and Manny go on holiday. But how will the three of them fare when they attempt to venture abroad together?
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
The Most Romantic One Ever: Groom Ash wants to pull off the most romantic wedding ever, but his bride-to-be Vicki shuns anything corny.
The Wet 'n' Wild Weymouth One: Leon wants to get wed on a beach in Weymouth. But bride-to-be Kirsty wants a fairy-tale day somewhere special to her.
The Halloween Special One: Sam wants a witchy wedding in the woods. But traditional bride Ezra has her heart set on a fairy-tale event.
Paul Damico, president of Moe's Southwest Grill, one of the world's fastest-growing restaurant chains, goes undercover to find out how efficient his organisation is.
Sara Bittorf, the chief brand officer of Boston Market - whose 470 restaurants serve home-style comfort food - goes undercover to work alongside the chain's employees.
In Marbella in Spain, three couples put their hosting skills to the test in a week featuring competitive piñata bashing, chicken critique and large helpings of frank feedback.
In and around Marbella in Spain, extrovert Jojo and Adrian's 1970s-inspired menu competes with fun-loving Mark and Zoe's international nosh and spiritual Caz and Dave's Jewish family feast.
In Birmingham, Veda and Varun kick off with a Bollywood bash, Rachel and Anna serve up home-cooked favourites, and Dave and Su plan a Mad Hatter's tea party with costumes to boot.
In Birmingham, competitive Corinne and Dal hope that their attention to detail will give them the edge. Jack and Georgie serve home-cooked favourites. And James and Adele do fine dining.
Sabatiello's: Gordon Ramsay sets his sights on Sabatiello's, an Italian restaurant in Connecticut that's better known for its owner Sammy's tantrums than the food.
Trobiano's: Gordon Ramsay meets struggling executive chef Anthony Trobiano, who's sinking under half a million dollars of debt at his restaurant in Great Neck, New York.
Jack's Waterfront: In Michigan, Gordon Ramsay visits a lakeside restaurant in a summer hotspot, where the food fails to match the fantastic views. Can Gordon turn the tide?
Hannah & Mason's: Gordon Ramsay's rescue mission this time is Hannah & Mason's, a French bistro in the quaint little town of Cranbury, New Jersey.
The Black Pearl: A struggling seafood restaurant in Manhattan, The Black Pearl, is hampered by debts, kitchen problems and three owners who all want to run things their way.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Amy Gledhill take on Richard Ayoade and Guz Khan. Sam Campbell joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
...at a Dog: Greg's hugely successful and acclaimed debut stand-up show from 2011, which was a huge hit at the Edinburgh Festival and went on a sell-out, UK-wide tour.
Manny Come Home: Fran comes back from holiday to discover that Manny has resigned and is now working next door at Goliath Books. And Bernard has let things slip a little...
Elephants and Hens: It's International Children's Book Week and Bernard and Manny, deciding they can do better than the trash that children are being offered, write their own.
Top moments from the comedy panel show. Jamali Maddix, Stacey Solomon, Ed Gamble, Kerry Godliman, Tom Read Wilson and many more join Jimmy Carr, Rob Beckett and Katherine Ryan.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Amy Gledhill take on Richard Ayoade and Guz Khan. Sam Campbell joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Manny Come Home: Fran comes back from holiday to discover that Manny has resigned and is now working next door at Goliath Books. And Bernard has let things slip a little...
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Undercover bosses Amit Kleinberger, Randy DeWitt and Steve Greenbaum take another look inside their firms, but this time they each send an employee undercover in their place.
Jeff Platt, the president and CEO of a chain of indoor trampoline parks, goes undercover in his own company, to find out whether his fast-growing business is running smoothly.
In Sheffield, there's bickering, failed romance and plenty of revelations, as car salesman Paul and his partner Sara, newly-weds Russell and Shane, and kooky duo Janine and Neil compete.
In Portsmouth, there's organic fare, glitter and sparkle, and some school dinner comparisons, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.
...Selling: Mike and Steve's cosy cottage is now home to four chaotic children too. They desperately need more room, but will a 200-year-old property be what they're looking for?
The One with the Wannabe King: Harry wants to be king for the day. But bride Charlotte wants something low key. Will this be Harry's crowning moment or will it be 'off with his head'?
The One at a Music Festival: Jake wants the ultimate wedding knees-up, but bride-to-be Rosie dreams of a more intimate affair. Will this festival wedding prove to be a headliner?
Armando Montelongo, CEO of Texas-based Armando Montelongo Companies, one of the leaders in real estate investment education in the US, goes incognito to work beside his staff.
Gina Butler is the founder and CEO of Gigi's Cupcakes, one of America's most successful bakeries, based in Nashville. She dons a disguise to go undercover among her workforce.
Finn McCool's: Gordon Ramsay visits Finn McCool's, a traditional Irish pub in the Hamptons, where the family feuding has got out of hand. Can Gordon mediate?
The Olde Stone Mill: Gordon Ramsay visits The Olde Stone Mill in Tuckahoe, New York. The restaurant has been restored, but owner Dean can't get customers through the door.
...and Selling: Brand new series: Beth and Chris want to move to a better district, but their house is aesthetically challenged - made worse when Jonathan uncovers bad news.
The Bitter End: The final night out goes as smoothly as you'd expect when things heat up as the crew say their final goodbyes.
There are fires, alarms, bizarre banquets and surprising sound effects, as our five comics complete tasks under the watchful eye of Greg Davies.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Harry Hill take on Jon Richardson and Rose Matafeo. Alex Horne and The Horne Section join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson take on Jon Richardson and Kiri Pritchard-McLean. The Brett Domino Trio join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Quantocking: Mark is worried he's drifting apart from Sophie, so immediate action is called for: he must propose... and where better than the Quantocks?
Sophie's Parents: Mark and Sophie are engaged to be married. Unfortunately Mark is having doubts; crippling doubts.
